Table of contents

  • Executive Summary
  • A. Calibration Studies
  • F. Large-N Bias Probes
  • B. Comparative Studies
  • C. Outlier and Heavy-Tailed Studies
  • D. Assumption Violation Studies
  • E. Edge Cases
  • H. Time Trend Distributional Misspecification
  • G. Bias Source Investigation
  • Convergence Diagnostics
  • Full Results Table

metadid Simulation Validation Report

Author

metadid-sims pipeline

Published

May 20, 2026

Code 
library(targets)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

tar_source("R/scenarios.R")
tar_source("R/plots.R")

all_agg <- tar_read(all_agg)
all_rep <- tar_read(all_rep)
scenario_lookup_tbl <- scenario_lookup()

Executive Summary

Code 
# Flag scenarios with empirical coverage below 80% for the treatment effect
poor_coverage <- all_agg |>
  filter(parameter == "treatment_effect_mean", empirical_coverage < 0.80)

convergence_issues <- all_agg |>
  filter(parameter == "treatment_effect_mean", pct_rhat_bad > 0.10)

64 scenarios evaluated across 50 replications each.

19 scenario(s) had coverage below 80% for the treatment effect mean.

5 scenario(s) had >10% of fits with convergence warnings.

Code 
kable(
  poor_coverage |>
    left_join(
      tibble(
        scenario_id = names(SCENARIO_CONFIGS),
        description = purrr::map_chr(SCENARIO_CONFIGS, "description")
      ),
      by = "scenario_id"
    ) |>
    select(scenario_id, description, model_label, empirical_coverage, mean_bias, rmse),
  digits = 3,
  caption = "Scenarios with <80% coverage for treatment_effect_mean"
)
Scenarios with <80% coverage for treatment_effect_mean
scenario_id description model_label empirical_coverage mean_bias rmse
F4 Large DiD + RCT only: 100 DiD + 100 RCT default 0.76 -0.001 0.008
B1 Naive vs full: PP-heavy, large trend (full should win) naive 0.00 -0.193 0.194
B3 Naive wins: DiD trend = 0, PP trend = -0.10 (pooled borrows wrong trend) full 0.24 -0.089 0.093
B3 Naive wins: DiD trend = 0, PP trend = -0.10 (pooled borrows wrong trend) naive 0.00 -0.114 0.116
B5 RCT baseline imbalance (only in this design): full (estimated) vs naive (fixed zero), 10 DiD + 10 RCT full 0.32 0.058 0.061
B5 RCT baseline imbalance (only in this design): full (estimated) vs naive (fixed zero), 10 DiD + 10 RCT naive 0.08 0.068 0.070
B6 DiD imbalance > RCT imbalance: full vs naive, 15 DiD + 15 RCT full 0.24 -0.047 0.051
B6 DiD imbalance > RCT imbalance: full vs naive, 15 DiD + 15 RCT naive 0.56 0.024 0.028
C6 Asymmetric contamination: outliers all biased in same direction normal 0.68 0.050 0.055
C6 Asymmetric contamination: outliers all biased in same direction robust 0.72 0.041 0.046
D4 Effect heterogeneity correlated with sample size default 0.00 0.126 0.127
D5 Baseline imbalance in RCT with baseline_imbalance=‘fixed_zero’ default 0.04 0.070 0.073
G8 Jensen’s test: baseline_sd = 0.10, amplified variation (200 DiD) default 0.32 -0.018 0.020
H1 Skewed trends: beta_i ~ -LogNormal (mean = -0.04, log-SD = 1), 15 DiD + 15 PP naive 0.08 -0.061 0.066
H2 Bimodal trends: 50/50 mixture N(-0.10, 0.01) + N(-0.01, 0.01), 15 DiD + 15 PP naive 0.16 -0.063 0.067
H3 Large trend variance: sigma_trend = 0.10 (5x default), 15 DiD + 15 PP naive 0.72 -0.046 0.055
H4 Trend-size confounding: DiD n ~ U(30,70), PP n ~ U(150,250), 15 DiD + 15 PP full_normal 0.44 0.041 0.046
H4 Trend-size confounding: DiD n ~ U(30,70), PP n ~ U(150,250), 15 DiD + 15 PP full_robust 0.40 0.041 0.047
H4 Trend-size confounding: DiD n ~ U(30,70), PP n ~ U(150,250), 15 DiD + 15 PP naive 0.72 -0.022 0.028

A. Calibration Studies

These scenarios test the model’s core calibration properties — coverage, bias, and RMSE — under a range of study counts, design mixes, and parameterisations. All use the full meta_did model unless noted. The baseline scenario (A1) uses 20 DiD studies with default parameters; subsequent scenarios probe how performance changes with sample size (A2–A4), mixed designs (A5–A6), covariates (A7), correlated effects (A8), unnormalised fitting (A9–A10), individual-level data (A11–A12), rho estimation strategies (A13), and sign/direction variants (A14–A18).

Code 
scenario_summary_table("A") |>
  kable(caption = "Category A: scenario definitions")
Category A: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
A1 Baseline: DiD-only summary, normalised, 20 studies (defaults) (defaults)
A2 Large sample: DiD-only, 60 studies n_did = 60 (defaults)
A3 Small sample: DiD-only, 5 studies n_did = 5 (defaults)
A4 Very large sample: DiD-only, 200 studies n_did = 200 (defaults)
A5 Mixed designs: 10 DiD + 10 RCT + 10 PP, normalised n_did = 10; n_rct = 10; n_pp = 10 (defaults)
A6 PP-heavy with trend: 5 DiD + 25 PP, trend = -0.10 n_did = 5; n_pp = 25; true_trend = -0.1; sigma_trend = 0.01 (defaults)
A7 Covariates: 30 DiD studies with dose covariate n_did = 30 (defaults)
A8 Correlated effects: 30 DiD, rho_effect_trend = 0.95 n_did = 30; baseline_sd = 0; rho_effect_trend = 0.95 fn = meta_did_general; correlated_effects = [TRUE]
A9 Unnormalised DiD-only, 20 studies, raw scale (defaults) normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]
A10 Unnormalised mixed: 10 DiD + 10 RCT + 10 PP, raw scale n_did = 10; n_rct = 10; n_pp = 10 normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]
A11 Individual-level DiD: fit from individual data, 20 studies (defaults) data_format = individual
A12 Individual vs summary consistency: same data, fit both ways, 20 studies (defaults) (defaults)
A13 Known vs hierarchical rho: same DiD data, compare rho modes (defaults) (defaults)
A14 Positive effect: true_effect = +0.15, trend = -0.04 true_effect = 0.15 (defaults)
A15 Null effect: true_effect = 0, trend = -0.04 true_effect = 0 (defaults)
A16 Positive trend: true_effect = -0.15, trend = +0.04 true_trend = 0.04 (defaults)
A17 Sign mismatch: negative effect (-0.15) with positive trend (+0.04), PP-heavy n_did = 10; n_pp = 20; true_trend = 0.04 (defaults)
A18 Both positive: true_effect = +0.15, trend = +0.04 true_effect = 0.15; true_trend = 0.04 (defaults)
Code 
A_combined    <- tar_read(A_combined)
A_agg_combined <- tar_read(A_agg_combined)
plot_diagnostics(A_combined, A_agg_combined, scenario_lookup_tbl, "A")
Code 
results_table(all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "A")
Category A: results
Scenario Description Model Fit Parameter Coverage Bias RMSE

F. Large-N Bias Probes

These scenarios use large numbers of studies (200 total) to narrow posteriors and expose any systematic bias that would be obscured in smaller samples. They vary the design mix, normalisation strategy, and model specification, allowing a stringent test of whether the model is asymptotically unbiased.

Code 
scenario_summary_table("F") |>
  kable(caption = "Category F: scenario definitions")
Category F: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
F1 Large mixed, normalised, full model: 70 DiD + 70 RCT + 60 PP n_did = 70; n_rct = 70; n_pp = 60 (defaults)
F2 Large mixed, normalised, naive: 70 DiD + 70 RCT + 60 PP (zero trend, equal baselines) n_did = 70; n_rct = 70; n_pp = 60; true_trend = 0; sigma_trend = 0; baseline_sd = 0 fn = meta_did_general; time_trend = fixed_zero; baseline_imbalance = fixed_zero
F3 Large mixed, unnormalised: 70 DiD + 70 RCT + 60 PP n_did = 70; n_rct = 70; n_pp = 60 normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]
F4 Large DiD + RCT only: 100 DiD + 100 RCT n_did = 100; n_rct = 100 (defaults)
F5 Large DiD + PP only: 100 DiD + 100 PP, nonzero trend n_did = 100; n_pp = 100; true_trend = -0.1; sigma_trend = 0.01 (defaults)
F6 Large DiD-only with covariates: 200 studies n_did = 200 (defaults)
F7 Large mixed, design effects: 70 DiD + 70 RCT + 60 PP with offsets n_did = 70; n_rct = 70; n_pp = 60 design_effects = [TRUE]
F8 Large DiD-only, correlated effects: 200 studies, rho_effect_trend = 0.95 n_did = 200; baseline_sd = 0; rho_effect_trend = 0.95 fn = meta_did_general; correlated_effects = [TRUE]
Code 
F_combined     <- tar_read(F_combined)
F_agg_combined <- tar_read(F_agg_combined)
plot_diagnostics(F_combined, F_agg_combined, scenario_lookup_tbl, "F")
Code 
results_table(all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "F")

B. Comparative Studies

These scenarios pit two model configurations against each other on the same simulated data, asking which recovers the treatment effect better under specific conditions. Each scenario has a clear hypothesis about which model should win (or whether they should tie).

Code 
scenario_summary_table("B") |>
  kable(caption = "Category B: scenario definitions")
Category B: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
B1 Naive vs full: PP-heavy, large trend (full should win) n_did = 5; n_pp = 25; true_trend = -0.1; sigma_trend = 0.01 (defaults)
B2 Naive vs full: assumptions satisfied (zero trend, equal baselines) n_did = 10; n_rct = 10; n_pp = 10; true_trend = 0; sigma_trend = 0; baseline_sd = 0 (defaults)
B3 Naive wins: DiD trend = 0, PP trend = -0.10 (pooled borrows wrong trend) n_did = 10; n_pp = 10; did_trend = 0; pp_trend = -0.1 (defaults)
B4 Correlated vs independent effects: rho_effect_trend = 0.95 n_did = 30; baseline_sd = 0; rho_effect_trend = 0.95 (defaults)
B5 RCT baseline imbalance (only in this design): full (estimated) vs naive (fixed zero), 10 DiD + 10 RCT n_did = 10; n_rct = 10; rct_gamma_mean = 0.05; rct_gamma_sd = 0.02 (defaults)
B6 DiD imbalance > RCT imbalance: full vs naive, 15 DiD + 15 RCT n_did = 15; n_rct = 15; did_gamma_mean = 0.08; did_gamma_sd = 0.02; rct_gamma_mean = 0.01; rct_gamma_sd = 0.01 (defaults)
Code 
plot_diagnostics(all_rep, all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "B")
Code 
results_table(all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "B")

C. Outlier and Heavy-Tailed Studies

These scenarios test robustness to departures from Gaussian study-level heterogeneity. The contaminating distributions are chosen to be detectable but not catastrophic, so that a robust model (Student-t heterogeneity) should outperform the normal model without being trivially obvious.

Code 
scenario_summary_table("C") |>
  kable(caption = "Category C: scenario definitions")
Category C: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
C1 Single outlier: one study shifted by 5*sigma_effect n_outlier = 1; outlier_shift = 5 (defaults)
C2 Multiple outliers: 3 of 20 from contaminating distribution n_outlier = 3; outlier_shift = 5 (defaults)
C3 Heavy-tailed study effects: theta_i ~ t(df=3) effect_df = 3 (defaults)
C4 Heavy-tailed within-study errors: observations ~ t(df=5) within_df = 5 (defaults)
C5 Outlier + small sample: single outlier among 5 studies n_did = 5; n_outlier = 1; outlier_shift = 5 (defaults)
C6 Asymmetric contamination: outliers all biased in same direction n_outlier = 3; outlier_shift = 5; outlier_direction = positive (defaults)
C7 Outlier in one design: 10 DiD + 10 PP, outliers only among PP n_did = 10; n_pp = 10; n_outlier_pp = 2; outlier_shift = 5 (defaults)
C8 Heavy-tailed time trends: beta_i ~ t(df=3) trend_df = 3 (defaults)
Code 
plot_diagnostics(all_rep, all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "C")
Code 
results_table(all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "C")
Category C: results
Scenario Description Model Fit Parameter Coverage Bias RMSE

normal time_trend_sd 0.80 0.009 0.014
normal treatment_effect_mean 0.84 -0.009 0.028
normal treatment_effect_sd 0.20 0.047 0.052
robust time_trend_mean 0.96 0.001 0.009
robust time_trend_sd 0.76 0.009 0.014
robust treatment_effect_mean 0.88 -0.009 0.024
robust treatment_effect_sd 0.48 0.031 0.037
C2 Multiple outliers: 3 of 20 from contaminating distribution normal time_trend_mean 0.84 0.002 0.012
normal time_trend_sd 0.88 0.005 0.011
normal treatment_effect_mean 0.84 -0.002 0.043
normal treatment_effect_sd 0.00 0.098 0.100
robust time_trend_mean 0.84 0.002 0.012
robust time_trend_sd 0.88 0.005 0.011
robust treatment_effect_mean 0.88 -0.001 0.035
robust treatment_effect_sd 0.12 0.070 0.071
C3 Heavy-tailed study effects: theta_i ~ t(df=3) normal time_trend_mean 0.92 -0.001 0.011
normal time_trend_sd 0.76 0.006 0.013
normal treatment_effect_mean 0.88 0.002 0.026
normal treatment_effect_sd 0.20 0.047 0.057
robust time_trend_mean 0.92 -0.001 0.011
robust time_trend_sd 0.80 0.006 0.013
robust treatment_effect_mean 0.88 0.004 0.025
robust treatment_effect_sd 0.60 0.036 0.044
C4 Heavy-tailed within-study errors: observations ~ t(df=5) normal time_trend_mean 0.92 -0.002 0.012
normal time_trend_sd 0.72 0.009 0.014
normal treatment_effect_mean 0.96 0.002 0.015
normal treatment_effect_sd 0.64 0.011 0.025
robust time_trend_mean 0.96 -0.002 0.012

D. Assumption Violation Studies

These scenarios introduce deliberate mismatches between the DGP and the fitted model to quantify the cost of specific assumption violations — unmodelled design effects, heterogeneous within-study variance, misspecified rho, effect-size/sample-size correlation, and ignored RCT baseline imbalance.

Code 
scenario_summary_table("D") |>
  kable(caption = "Category D: scenario definitions")
Category D: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
D1 Design-specific effects ignored: RCT/PP shifted, design_effects=FALSE n_did = 10; n_rct = 10; n_pp = 10; delta_rct = 0.1; delta_pp = -0.08 (defaults)
D2 Heterogeneous within-study variance: sigma_within drawn from Gamma within_sd_shape = 4; within_sd_rate = 33.3333333333333 (defaults)
D3 Misspecified rho: true rho varies by study (Beta distribution) rho_alpha = 5; rho_beta = 5 (defaults)
D4 Effect heterogeneity correlated with sample size size_effect_slope = 0.001 (defaults)
D5 Baseline imbalance in RCT with baseline_imbalance=‘fixed_zero’ n_did = 10; n_rct = 10; rct_gamma_mean = 0.05; rct_gamma_sd = 0.02 fn = meta_did_general; baseline_imbalance = fixed_zero
Code 
D_combined     <- tar_read(D_combined)
D_agg_combined <- tar_read(D_agg_combined)
plot_diagnostics(D_combined, D_agg_combined, scenario_lookup_tbl, "D")
Code 
results_table(all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "D")

E. Edge Cases

These scenarios probe boundary conditions: extreme pre-post correlations, zero heterogeneity, very large heterogeneity, unbalanced arms, single studies per design, and missing rho values. They test whether the model remains stable and well-calibrated when inputs approach the limits of the assumed data-generating regime.

Code 
scenario_summary_table("E") |>
  kable(caption = "Category E: scenario definitions")
Category E: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
E1 Extreme rho: pre-post correlation near 0 and near 1 rho_values = [0.02, 0.98] (defaults)
E2 Zero heterogeneity: all studies have identical true effect sigma_effect = 0; sigma_trend = 0; baseline_sd = 0 (defaults)
E3 Large heterogeneity: tau >> typical effect size sigma_effect = 0.3; sigma_trend = 0.1 (defaults)
E4 Unbalanced arms: n_treatment = 200, n_control = 30 n_control = 30; n_treatment = 200 (defaults)
E5 Single study per design: 1 DiD + 1 RCT + 1 PP n_did = 1; n_rct = 1; n_pp = 1 (defaults)
E6 Missing rho: hierarchical_rho imputes (defaults) provide_rho = [FALSE]
Code 
E_combined     <- tar_read(E_combined)
E_agg_combined <- tar_read(E_agg_combined)
plot_diagnostics(E_combined, E_agg_combined, scenario_lookup_tbl, "E", exclude = "E5")
Code 
results_table(all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "E")
Category E: results
Scenario Description Model Fit Parameter Coverage Bias RMSE

default treatment_effect_mean 0.92 0.009 0.131
default treatment_effect_sd 0.92 0.049 0.119
E4 Unbalanced arms: n_treatment = 200, n_control = 30 default time_trend_mean 0.96 0.001 0.014
default time_trend_sd 0.84 0.008 0.018
default treatment_effect_mean 0.92 -0.021 0.028
default treatment_effect_sd 0.56 0.026 0.033

default time_trend_sd 0.72 1.179 1.301
default treatment_effect_mean 1.00 0.044 0.212
default treatment_effect_sd 1.00 0.759 0.826
E6 Missing rho: hierarchical_rho imputes default time_trend_mean 0.88 -0.001 0.010
default time_trend_sd 0.80 0.003 0.014
default treatment_effect_mean 0.88 -0.003 0.021
default treatment_effect_sd 0.76 0.015 0.023

H. Time Trend Distributional Misspecification

The full model assumes study-level time trends are exchangeable draws from a normal distribution. These scenarios test what happens when that assumption is violated at the population level, using a 15 DiD + 15 PP design so that PP studies — which rely on trend estimation for effect identification — are directly exposed to the misspecification. Each scenario compares three models on the same simulated data: the full normal model (meta_did), the full robust model (meta_did with Student-t heterogeneity), and the naive model (meta_did_general with time_trend = "fixed_zero").

H1 generates trends from a negated log-normal (mean = −0.04, log-SD = 1), producing strong right-skewness: most studies have trends close to zero with occasional large negative trends. H2 uses a 50/50 mixture of two normal components centred at −0.01 and −0.10 — a bimodal distribution the normal model cannot represent regardless of sample size. H3 keeps the normal DGP but inflates sigma_trend to 0.10 (five times the default), testing the regime where trend uncertainty dominates effect uncertainty. H4 introduces a design-confounded trend: DiD studies are small (n ~ U(30, 70)) and have more negative trends, while PP studies are large (n ~ U(150, 250)) and have trends closer to zero; because the model pools trends across designs, it over-adjusts PP outcomes.

Code 
scenario_summary_table("H") |>
  kable(caption = "Category H: scenario definitions")
Category H: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
H1 Skewed trends: beta_i ~ -LogNormal (mean = -0.04, log-SD = 1), 15 DiD + 15 PP n_did = 15; n_pp = 15; sigma_trend = 0.052; lognormal_sigma = 1 (defaults)
H2 Bimodal trends: 50/50 mixture N(-0.10, 0.01) + N(-0.01, 0.01), 15 DiD + 15 PP n_did = 15; n_pp = 15; true_trend = -0.055; sigma_trend = 0.046; trend_low = -0.1; trend_high = -0.01; mix_prob = 0.5; sigma_within = 0.01 (defaults)
H3 Large trend variance: sigma_trend = 0.10 (5x default), 15 DiD + 15 PP n_did = 15; n_pp = 15; sigma_trend = 0.1 (defaults)
H4 Trend-size confounding: DiD n ~ U(30,70), PP n ~ U(150,250), 15 DiD + 15 PP n_did = 15; n_pp = 15; trend_size_slope = 3e-04; n_small_min = 30; n_small_max = 70; n_large_min = 150; n_large_max = 250 (defaults)
Code 
plot_diagnostics(all_rep, all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "H")
Code 
results_table(all_agg, scenario_lookup_tbl, "H")

G. Bias Source Investigation

Category G probes the two systematic bias sources identified in the large-N results (Category F). It comprises two sub-investigations.

G1–G5: Prior sensitivity (unnormalised model). The unnormalised model showed a small positive bias (~0.6–0.9%) for a negative true effect, consistent with hierarchical shrinkage toward zero from the default N(0, 10) prior on treatment_effect_mean. G1–G5 vary the prior standard deviation from 1 to 100 (G3 replicates the default) to test whether the bias decreases monotonically as the prior becomes more diffuse.

G6–G9: Jensen’s inequality (normalised model). The normalised model showed ~1% systematic downward bias at 200 studies, consistent with Jensen’s inequality on the ratio estimator: because the model fits E[θ/baseline] rather than E[θ]/E[baseline], the convexity of the reciprocal function induces a negative shift proportional to between-study baseline variance. G6–G8 vary between-study baseline variation (baseline_sd = 0, 0.01, 0.10) to confirm that the bias tracks the Jensen term; G9 uses a very large control group (n_control = 5000) to test whether high within-study precision alone eliminates the bias.

Code 
scenario_summary_table("G") |>
  kable(caption = "Category G: scenario definitions")
Category G: scenario definitions
ID Description DGP overrides Fit overrides
G1 Prior sensitivity: effect/trend prior sd = 1 (unnormalised, 20 DiD) (defaults) normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]; priors = [list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 1), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 1), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “gamma”, shape = 2, rate = 0.1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “lkj”, eta = 2), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 0.1)]
G2 Prior sensitivity: effect/trend prior sd = 5 (unnormalised, 20 DiD) (defaults) normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]; priors = [list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 5), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 5), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “gamma”, shape = 2, rate = 0.1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “lkj”, eta = 2), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 0.1)]
G3 Prior sensitivity: effect/trend prior sd = 10 (default, unnormalised, 20 DiD) (defaults) normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]; priors = [list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “gamma”, shape = 2, rate = 0.1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “lkj”, eta = 2), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 0.1)]
G4 Prior sensitivity: effect/trend prior sd = 50 (unnormalised, 20 DiD) (defaults) normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]; priors = [list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 50), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 50), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “gamma”, shape = 2, rate = 0.1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “lkj”, eta = 2), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 0.1)]
G5 Prior sensitivity: effect/trend prior sd = 100 (unnormalised, 20 DiD) (defaults) normalise_by_baseline = [FALSE]; priors = [list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 100), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 100), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “gamma”, shape = 2, rate = 0.1), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 5), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 10), list(dist = “lkj”, eta = 2), list(dist = “normal”, mean = 0, sd = 0.5), list(dist = “cauchy”, scale = 0.1)]
G6 Jensen’s test: baseline_sd = 0, no between-study variation (200 DiD) n_did = 200; baseline_sd = 0 (defaults)
G7 Jensen’s test: baseline_sd = 0.01, reduced variation (200 DiD) n_did = 200; baseline_sd = 0.01 (defaults)
G8 Jensen’s test: baseline_sd = 0.10, amplified variation (200 DiD) n_did = 200; baseline_sd = 0.1 (defaults)
G9 Jensen’s test: n_control = 5000, high within-study precision (200 DiD) n_did = 200; n_control = 5000 (defaults)

Convergence Diagnostics

Code 
plot_convergence(all_agg)
